logical_units.title: A. P. (DIR Series) Circular No. 20 December 12, 2022 (Updated as on April 15, 2024)
logical_units.text:
A. P. (DIR Series) Circular No. 20 December 12, 2022 (Updated as on April 15, 2024)

All Authorised Dealer Category - I Banks

Madam / Sir,
Master Direction - Foreign Exchange Management (Hedging of Commodity Price Risk
and Freight Risk in Overseas Markets) Directions, 2022
Attention of Authorised Dealer Category - I (AD Cat-I) banks is invited to Regulation 6 and 6A
of the Foreign Exchange Management (Foreign Exchange Derivative Contracts) Regulations,
2000 dated May 3, 2000 (Notification No. FEMA. 25/RB-2000 dated May 3, 2000), as
amended from time to time, issued under clause (h) of sub-section (2) of Section 47 of Foreign
Exchange Management Act, 1999 (Act 42 of 1999) as amended from time to time.

logical_units.title: 2. Within the contours of the Regulations, the Reserve Bank issues directions to Authorised
logical_units.text:
2. Within the contours of the Regulations, the Reserve Bank issues directions to Authorised
Persons under Section 11 of the Foreign Exchange Management Act, 1999 (Act 42 of 1999).
These Directions lay down the modalities for the AD Cat-I banks for facilitating hedging of
commodity price risk and freight risk in overseas markets by their customers / constituents.

logical_units.title: 3. The Master Direction - Foreign Exchange Management (Hedging of Commodity Price Risk
logical_units.text:
3. The Master Direction - Foreign Exchange Management (Hedging of Commodity Price Risk
and Freight Risk in Overseas Markets) Directions, 2022 are enclosed herewith. AD Cat-I
banks may bring the contents of these Directions to the notice of their customers / constituents
concerned.

logical_units.title: 4. The directions contained in this circular have been issued under Sections 10(4) and 11(1)
logical_units.text:
4. The directions contained in this circular have been issued under Sections 10(4) and 11(1)
of the Foreign Exchange Management Act, 1999 (42 of 1999) and are without prejudice to
permissions/ approvals, if any, required under any other law.
Yours faithfully,
(Dimple Bhandia) Chief General Manager

logical_units.title: A. P. (DIR Series) Circular No. 21 dated December 12, 2022
logical_units.text:
A. P. (DIR Series) Circular No. 21 dated December 12, 2022
Master Direction - Foreign Exchange Management (Hedging of Commodity
Price Risk and Freight Risk in Overseas Markets) Directions, 2022
The Reserve Bank of India, in exercise of the powers conferred under Sections 10(4)
and 11(1) of the Foreign Exchange Management Act (FEMA), 1999 (42 of 1999),
hereby issues the following Directions.

logical_units.title: 1. Short title and commencement
logical_units.text: 1. Short title and commencement

logical_units.title: (i) These Directions shall be called the Master Direction - Foreign Exchange
logical_units.text:
(i) These Directions shall be called the Master Direction - Foreign Exchange
Management (Hedging of Commodity Price Risk and Freight Risk in Overseas
Markets) Directions, 2022.

logical_units.title: (ii) These Directions shall come into force on December 12, 2022.
logical_units.text: (ii) These Directions shall come into force on December 12, 2022.

logical_units.title: 2. Definitions
logical_units.text: 2. Definitions

logical_units.title: (i) Hedging - The activity of undertaking a derivative transaction to reduce an
logical_units.text:
(i) Hedging - The activity of undertaking a derivative transaction to reduce an
identifiable and measurable risk. For the purpose of these directions, the relevant risks
are commodity price risk and freight risk.

logical_units.title: (ii) Eligible entities - Eligible entities refers to residents other than Individuals.
logical_units.text: (ii) Eligible entities - Eligible entities refers to residents other than Individuals.

logical_units.title: (iii) Direct Exposure to Commodity Price Risk - An eligible entity will be said to have
logical_units.text:
(iii) Direct Exposure to Commodity Price Risk - An eligible entity will be said to have
direct exposure to commodity price risk if

logical_units.title: (a) It purchases/sells a commodity (in India or abroad) whose price is fixed by
logical_units.text:
(a) It purchases/sells a commodity (in India or abroad) whose price is fixed by
reference to an international benchmark; or

logical_units.title: (b) It purchases/sells a product (in India or abroad) which contains a commodity
logical_units.text:
(b) It purchases/sells a product (in India or abroad) which contains a commodity
and the price of the product is linked to an international benchmark of the
commodity.

logical_units.title: (iv) Indirect Exposure to Commodity Price Risk - An eligible entity will be said to have
logical_units.text:
(iv) Indirect Exposure to Commodity Price Risk - An eligible entity will be said to have
indirect exposure to commodity price risk if it purchases/sells a product (in India or
abroad) which contains the commodity and the price of the product is not linked to an
international benchmark of the commodity.

logical_units.title: (v) Exposure to Freight Risk - An eligible entity will be said to have exposure to freight
logical_units.text:
(v) Exposure to Freight Risk - An eligible entity will be said to have exposure to freight
risk if it is engaged in the business of refining oil or is engaged in the business of
shipping.

logical_units.title: (vi) Bank(s) - Bank(s) refer to banks licensed as Authorised Dealer - Category I under
logical_units.text:
(vi) Bank(s) - Bank(s) refer to banks licensed as Authorised Dealer - Category I under
Section 10 of FEMA, 1999.
<image>

logical_units.title: (vii) 'International Financial Service Centre' shall have the same meaning as assigned
logical_units.text:
(vii) 'International Financial Service Centre' shall have the same meaning as assigned
to it in the Section 2(q) of the Special Economic Zones Act, 2005.

logical_units.title: 3. Eligible commodities - Commodities whose price risk may be hedged are:
logical_units.text: 3. Eligible commodities - Commodities whose price risk may be hedged are:

logical_units.title: (i) In case of direct exposures to commodity price risk: All commodities (except Gems
logical_units.text:
(i) In case of direct exposures to commodity price risk: All commodities (except Gems
and Precious stones). Price risk of gold may only be hedged as provided at Para 5 (ii)
of these directions.

logical_units.title: (ii) In case of indirect exposures to commodity price risk: Aluminium, Copper, Lead,
logical_units.text:
(ii) In case of indirect exposures to commodity price risk: Aluminium, Copper, Lead,
Zinc, Nickel, and Tin. This list of eligible commodities would be reviewed annually.

logical_units.title: 4. Permitted products - Permitted products refer to the following:
logical_units.text: 4. Permitted products - Permitted products refer to the following:

logical_units.title: (i) Generic Products
logical_units.text: (i) Generic Products

logical_units.title: (a) Futures and forwards
logical_units.text: (a) Futures and forwards

logical_units.title: (b) Vanilla options (call option and put option)
logical_units.text: (b) Vanilla options (call option and put option)

logical_units.title: (c) Swaps
logical_units.text: (c) Swaps

logical_units.title: (ii) Structured Products
logical_units.text: (ii) Structured Products

logical_units.title: (a) Products which are combination of either cash instrument and one or more
logical_units.text:
(a) Products which are combination of either cash instrument and one or more
generic products

logical_units.title: (b) Products which are combination of two or more generic products
logical_units.text: (b) Products which are combination of two or more generic products

logical_units.title: 5. Hedging of commodity price risk
logical_units.text: 5. Hedging of commodity price risk

logical_units.title: (i) Eligible entities having exposure to commodity price risk for any eligible commodity
logical_units.text:
(i) Eligible entities having exposure to commodity price risk for any eligible commodity
may hedge such exposure in overseas markets using any of the permitted products.

logical_units.title: (ii) Eligible entities having exposure to price risk of gold may hedge such exposure in
logical_units.text:
(ii) Eligible entities having exposure to price risk of gold may hedge such exposure in
the International Financial Services Centre (IFSC), subject to the stipulations set out
in this Master Direction.

logical_units.title: 6. Hedging of freight risk: Eligible entities having exposure to freight risk may hedge
logical_units.text:
6. Hedging of freight risk: Eligible entities having exposure to freight risk may hedge
such exposure in overseas markets by using any of the permitted products.

logical_units.title: 7. Other operational guidelines
logical_units.text: 7. Other operational guidelines

logical_units.title: (i) Banks may permit eligible entities to hedge commodity price risk and freight risk
logical_units.text:
(i) Banks may permit eligible entities to hedge commodity price risk and freight risk
overseas, including IFSC, using permitted products and may remit foreign exchange
in respect of such transactions after satisfying themselves that:

logical_units.title: (a) The entity has exposure to commodity price risk or freight risk, contracted
logical_units.text:
(a) The entity has exposure to commodity price risk or freight risk, contracted
or anticipated.
<image>

logical_units.title: (b) The quantity proposed to be hedged and the tenor of the hedge are in line
logical_units.text:
(b) The quantity proposed to be hedged and the tenor of the hedge are in line
with the exposure.

logical_units.title: (c) In case of OTC derivatives, the requirement to undertake OTC hedges is
logical_units.text:
(c) In case of OTC derivatives, the requirement to undertake OTC hedges is
justified.

logical_units.title: (d) In case of hedging using a benchmark price other than that of the commodity
logical_units.text:
(d) In case of hedging using a benchmark price other than that of the commodity
exposed to, the requirement to undertake such hedges is justified.

logical_units.title: (e) Such hedging is taken up by the management of the entity under a policy
logical_units.text:
(e) Such hedging is taken up by the management of the entity under a policy
approved by the Board of Directors of a company or equivalent forum for other.

logical_units.title: (f) The entity has the necessary risk management policies in place.
logical_units.text: (f) The entity has the necessary risk management policies in place.

logical_units.title: (g) The entity has reasonable understanding of the utility and likely risks
logical_units.text:
(g) The entity has reasonable understanding of the utility and likely risks
associated with the products proposed to be used for hedging.

logical_units.title: (ii) OTC contracts shall be booked with a bank or with non-bank entities which are
logical_units.text:
(ii) OTC contracts shall be booked with a bank or with non-bank entities which are
permitted to offer such derivatives by their regulators. For this purpose, a list of
acceptable jurisdictions shall be specified by FEDAI.

logical_units.title: (iii) Structured products may be permitted to eligible entities who are (a) listed on
logical_units.text:
(iii) Structured products may be permitted to eligible entities who are (a) listed on
recognized domestic stock exchanges or (b) fully owned subsidiaries of such entities
or (c) unlisted entities whose net worth is higher than INR 200 crore, subject to the
condition that such product are used for the purpose of hedging as defined under these
directions.

logical_units.title: (iv) All payments/receipts related to hedging of exposure to commodity price risk and
logical_units.text:
(iv) All payments/receipts related to hedging of exposure to commodity price risk and
freight risk shall be routed through a special account with the bank for this purpose.

logical_units.title: (v) Banks shall keep on their records full details of all hedge transactions and related
logical_units.text:
(v) Banks shall keep on their records full details of all hedge transactions and related
remittances made by the entity.

logical_units.title: (vi) Banks shall obtain an annual certificate from the statutory auditors of the entity
logical_units.text:
(vi) Banks shall obtain an annual certificate from the statutory auditors of the entity
confirming that the hedge transactions and the margin remittances are in line with the
exposure of the entity. The statutory auditor shall also comment on the risk
management policy of the entity for hedging exposure to commodity price risk and
freight risk and the appropriateness of the methodology to arrive at the quantum of
these exposures.

logical_units.title: (vii) Banks shall undertake immediate corrective action in case of any irregularity or
logical_units.text:
(vii) Banks shall undertake immediate corrective action in case of any irregularity or
misuse of these Directions. All such cases should be reported to the Chief General
Manager, Financial Markets Regulation Department, Reserve Bank of India.
<image>

logical_units.title: 8. Standby Letters of Credit (SBLC) / Guarantees: Banks are permitted to issue
logical_units.text:
8. Standby Letters of Credit (SBLC) / Guarantees: Banks are permitted to issue
Standby Letters of Credit (SBLC) / Guarantees, for a maximum period of one year, on
behalf of their clients in lieu of making a remittance of margin money for commodity
hedging transactions entered into by their customers. Banks should ensure that these
SBLCs / Guarantees are used by their clients for the intended purposes.

logical_units.title: 9. Realisation and repatriation of foreign exchange: Realisation and repatriation of
logical_units.text:
9. Realisation and repatriation of foreign exchange: Realisation and repatriation of
foreign exchange due or accruing to an eligible entity resulting from permitted
transactions under this direction shall be guided by the provisions of the Foreign
Exchange Management (Realisation, repatriation and surrender of foreign exchange)
Regulations, 2015.

logical_units.title: 10. Report to Reserve Bank: Banks shall submit a quarterly report to the Chief
logical_units.text:
10. Report to Reserve Bank: Banks shall submit a quarterly report to the Chief
General Manager, Financial Markets Regulation Department, Reserve Bank of India
through Centralised Information Management System (CIMS) in the format provided
in Annexure I by the 15th of the month following the end of the quarter. In case of no
transactions, a "Nil" report shall be submitted by the bank.

logical_units.title: 11. Repeal
logical_units.text:
11. Repeal
The following circulars issued by the Reserve Bank stand repealed as on the date on
which these Directions come into force:

logical_units.title: (i) A. P. (DIR Series) Circular No. 19 dated March 12, 2018 on Hedging of Commodity
logical_units.text:
(i) A. P. (DIR Series) Circular No. 19 dated March 12, 2018 on Hedging of Commodity
Price Risk and Freight Risk in Overseas Markets.

logical_units.title: (ii) A. P. (DIR Series) Circular No. 16 dated January 15, 2020 on Hedging of
logical_units.text:
(ii) A. P. (DIR Series) Circular No. 16 dated January 15, 2020 on Hedging of
Commodity Price Risk and Freight Risk in Overseas Markets - Amendment.
